admin.title: Admin Panel

appStoreAdmin.subtitle: Review and manage third-party app submissions.
appStoreAdmin.rejectDesc: The developer receives this reason by email and can resubmit.
appStoreAdmin.rejectPlaceholder: e.g. Privacy policy URL returns 404; please fix and resubmit.
appStoreAdmin.rejectedNote: Rejected apps show the reason the developer received by email.
appStoreAdmin.forceConfirm: Approve without origin verification — only for a trusted Sentroy-curated app (you can't verify {origin}).

editorsChoice.title: Editor's Choice
editorsChoice.subtitle: Curate the apps featured at the top of the App Store. Order matters — the first ones show first.
editorsChoice.empty: No featured apps yet. Add some from the list below.

systemProductsPage.title: One-time products
systemProductsPage.subtitle: Fixed-amount products that first-party apps can sell as one-time purchases.
systemProductsPage.hint: Create a one-time product in your Polar dashboard for each amount, then paste their product IDs here. Sandbox and production are kept separate; the amounts themselves are fixed in code.
systemProductsPage.usage: Apps start a purchase with POST /api/billing/system-checkout { amount, app, reference } and verify payment with GET /api/billing/system-purchases.

admin.seoAnalyticsDesc: Tracking script IDs. Leave any field blank to disable that provider — only set IDs are emitted into the page head.
admin.seoVerificationDesc: Search engine verification tokens emitted as <meta> tags in the document head.
admin.seoDefaultMetaDesc: Per-locale fallback title, description, and keywords used whenever a page does not supply its own metadata.
admin.seoDefaultKeywordsHint: Comma-separated. Used as the <meta name="keywords"> fallback.
admin.seoSocialDesc: Defaults used by Open Graph and Twitter card meta.
admin.seoTwitterHandleHint: Without the leading @ — emitted as twitter:site / twitter:creator.
admin.seoRobotsDesc: Replaces the auto-generated robots.txt. Leave empty to keep the platform default. One directive per line.
admin.noCompanies: This user is not a member of any company yet
admin.resetLinkConfirm: Send a password reset link to {email}?
admin.resetLinkDesc: Sends a password reset link to the user's email address.
admin.impersonateConfirm: You will be signed in as {name}. Your current admin session will be replaced with this user's session.
admin.impersonateDesc: Opens this user's session so you can view the panel as them. The action is recorded in the audit log.
admin.defaultLimitsDescription: Default limits applied to new accounts and companies.
admin.name: Name
admin.deleteCompanyConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This will also remove all members.
admin.polarMappingHint: Map this plan to Polar product IDs. Sandbox and production are separate; the active environment is selected under Admin → Billing.
admin.applicablePlanIds: Applicable Plan IDs (comma separated, empty = all)
admin.storageSettingsDesc: Single-file upload size cap applied across the storage app and CDN proxy.
admin.maxUploadBytesHint: Files larger than this are rejected at upload time. Default 50 MB. Note: the CDN container's MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES env should be ≥ this value.
admin.authLimitsDesc: Per-IP rate limits and token lifetimes for sign-in flows. Configured in the auth server.
admin.authLimitsNote: Edit packages/auth/src/server/auth.ts to change limits or token lifetime.

cm.title: Contact messages
cm.subtitle: Messages from the contact form.
cm.noEmail: No email — reply cannot be sent

projects.selectPrompt: Select or create a project to manage its environment variables.
projects.createDescription: A project corresponds to a single application of yours (e.g., my-app, my-blog).
projects.deleteDescription: All environments, variables, and tokens will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.

variables.deleteDescription: "{key}" will be removed from {environment}. This action cannot be undone.
variables.createDescription: Public variables can be read by the React useEnv() hook from the browser. Server-only secrets stay server-side.
variables.publicHint: The useEnv() hook passes the secret to the browser; enable it if it isn't a server-only secret.
variables.editDescription: Edit this variable in the {environment} environment.
variables.decryptWarning: Decrypt failed. The master key may have changed; type a new value and save to re-encrypt with the current key.

developerMode.description: Edit every variable in {environment} as a .env file. Add a description on the line above a key, and write a single "# @public" line to expose it to the browser. Save applies the diff in one shot.
developerMode.deleteConfirmDescription: {keys} will be removed from {environment}. This action cannot be undone.
developerMode.exitConfirmDescription: You have unsaved edits in the .env editor. Discard them?

tokens.empty: No tokens. Tokens are scoped to (project, environment).
tokens.revokeDescription: Revoking the token will stop deploys using this credential from fetching envs. This cannot be undone.
tokens.createDescription: A token is bound to a (project, environment) pair.
tokens.writePermissionHint: When off, the token can only fetch envs; turn it on for CLI publish.
tokens.createdDescription: This token will not be shown again. Copy it now and store it somewhere safe.
tokens.createdHint: Set this token as SENTROY_ENV_API_KEY in your deploy environment.

systemEnvs.title: Sentroy system envs
systemEnvs.description: Migrated env keys used by Sentroy itself. Each row shows whether the value is currently being read from the vault, from process.env (deploy fallback), or is missing entirely.
systemEnvs.summary: {inVault} of {total} in vault · {processEnv} on process.env · {missing} missing
systemEnvs.loadFailed: Failed to load system env status.
systemEnvs.tooltipUnknown: This env is read by another app (e.g. mail). Diagnostic running in core can't see its process.env.

webhooks.empty: No webhooks. Create one to invalidate app caches the moment a variable changes.
webhooks.createDescription: Sentroy will POST to this URL whenever any variable in the chosen environment changes. The payload is signed with HMAC-SHA256 — verify it on the receiver with the secret shown after creation.
webhooks.createdDescription: This secret will not be shown again. Copy it now and set it in the receiver app as SENTROY_VAULT_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
webhooks.deleteDescription: Variable changes in this scope will no longer notify {url}. This cannot be undone.
webhooks.toggleHint: Off to pause delivery without deleting the webhook.

connectedApps.title: Connected apps
connectedApps.subtitle: Sentroy Auth
connectedApps.description: Apps you've signed into with your Sentroy account. Revoke any of them and they'll need your permission again.
connectedApps.empty: You haven't signed into anything with your Sentroy account yet.
connectedApps.revokeConfirmDescription: The app will be signed out immediately and any active tokens it holds will stop working. To use it again you'll be asked to re-authorize.

systemStatus.title: System Status
systemStatus.description: Live health probe of MongoDB, mail server, CDN, and internal apps.
systemStatus.restartConfirmDesc: Restarts only the {service} container on Coolify. Other resources are unaffected. Expect 30-60 seconds of downtime while the container cold-starts.
systemStatus.restartTriggered: {service} restart requested — give it ~60s to come back.

templateLibrary.title: Template library
templateLibrary.description: Curated email templates by category. Public ones surface in user template browse.
templateLibrary.templatesDesc: Add, edit, organize templates that companies can clone into their own catalogue.
templateLibrary.empty: No templates yet. Add the first one to seed the library.
templateLibrary.deleteDesc: "{key}" will be removed from the library. Companies that already cloned it keep their copy.
templateLibrary.dialogDesc: Compose with MJML; subject and body support per-locale variants.
templateLibrary.name: Name
templateLibrary.subject: Subject
templateLibrary.htmlRequired: HTML body is required for the default locale
templateLibrary.variablesHint: Comma-separated. Used by clone preview to show placeholders.
templateLibrary.variablesAutoHint: Auto-derived from '{var}' placeholders in subject and body.
templateLibrary.variablesEmpty: No variables yet — add '{example}' to subject or body.
templateLibrary.collectionsDesc: Group templates that share a design language. Users can browse the full collection and clone all at once.
templateLibrary.noCollections: No collections yet — templates will be standalone.
templateLibrary.deleteCollectionDesc: "{key}" will be removed; templates in it become standalone (not deleted).
templateLibrary.collectionDialogDesc: Group identifier shown in user library — same template family across categories.
templateLibrary.coverUrlHint: Optional. Shown on the collection card in the user library.

systemMail.title: System mail
systemMail.description: Domains and sender used by automated platform emails (verification, password reset, OTP).
systemMail.fromAddressDesc: Local part used as the sender (e.g. noreply@yourdomain).
systemMail.domainsDesc: Add a domain, complete DNS records, then set it as the active system mail domain.
systemMail.dnsHint: Add these records at your DNS provider, then click Verify.
systemMail.deleteConfirm: Delete {domain} from the system mail domain pool?
systemMail.empty: No system mail domain yet. Add one above.
systemMail.assignDialogDesc: Members of the selected company will manage mailboxes, templates, and inbox for {domain} as if it were their own. DKIM records stay the same.
systemMail.noCompaniesToAssign: No assignable companies — create or provision one first.
systemMail.unassignConfirm: Remove assignment for {domain}? Domain returns to system pool.
systemMail.eventsDesc: Customise the subject and HTML body of every transactional system mail (verification, password reset, OTP, invitations). Save to override; reset to drop back to the built-in defaults.
systemMail.eventsListDesc: Pick an event to edit its content per locale.
systemMail.usingDefaults: Using built-in defaults — your edits will create an override.
systemMail.resetDialogDesc: This deletes the override for "{event}" and the built-in template kicks back in. The next send will use platform defaults.
systemMail.emptyEditorDesc: Select a transactional mail from the list to view and edit its subject and body.
systemMail.templatePickerDesc: Pick an existing mail template to seed this event's subject + body. The variable diff below will warn about missing or extra placeholders.
systemMail.templatePickerSearchPlaceholder: Search by name, subject, key or category…
systemMail.templateApplied: Template applied — review the variable diff
systemMail.variableDiffMissing: {count} expected variable(s) not used in template — they won't appear in the message:
systemMail.variableDiffExtra: {count} placeholder(s) the system will not provide — they'll render literally or as empty:

ai.label: AI
ai.bodyRequired: Write something first to enhance or change tone

aiCompose.title: Generate with AI
aiCompose.description: Describe what this template should do — pick an existing one as a style reference if you'd like.
aiCompose.promptPlaceholder: e.g. OTP verification code email with brand voice and a 6-digit placeholder
aiCompose.exampleHint: AI will match the tone and structure of the chosen template.
aiCompose.modelHint: Different models trade off speed, cost, and quality. Pricing per 1M tokens.
aiCompose.notesPlaceholder: e.g. Keep it under 80 words, formal tone
aiCompose.applied: AI draft applied to the editor
aiCompose.generateFailed: Couldn't generate. Try a different prompt or fewer languages.

collectionAiFill.title: AI fill collection
collectionAiFill.description: Generate one welcome template, then auto-fill the rest of the collection in matching style.
collectionAiFill.descriptionFor: Fill the "{collection}" collection — start with a welcome mail, then sweep the remaining categories in matching style.
collectionAiFill.logoUrlHint: Used in the header. The template still works for brands without a logo — text fallback is included.
collectionAiFill.generateFailed: Couldn't generate. Try a different prompt or model.
collectionAiFill.anchorExplain: Approve to lock this in as the style anchor — the wizard will sweep the remaining categories using it as a style guide.
collectionAiFill.sweepRunning: Generating each category in matching style. This can take a minute or two on slower models.
collectionAiFill.doneAllGood: Every category landed cleanly. You can edit any of them from the grid.
collectionAiFill.doneWithFailures: {failed} category/categories failed — you can retry by editing the chip and running the wizard again.

backupsAdmin.title: Database backups
backupsAdmin.description: Snapshot the current MongoDB cluster to a remote URI. Run on demand, retry, or restore an older snapshot back into production.
backupsAdmin.newBackupDesc: Connects to the target cluster and writes a fresh database (sentroy-backup-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS) with all current collections.
backupsAdmin.empty: No backup jobs yet. Create one to start a snapshot.
backupsAdmin.importHint: Take a snapshot of the current DB first, then apply the uploaded dump.
backupsAdmin.restoreDesc: This will overwrite the current cluster's database with the snapshot {dbName} from {host}.
backupsAdmin.restoreWarning: DESTRUCTIVE: every collection in the current database will be dropped and replaced. Make a fresh backup first if needed.
backupsAdmin.restoreCustomTargetHint: Default: write to the current MONGODB_URI. Provide a different mongodb:// URI to copy this snapshot elsewhere (dev clone, fail-over).
backupsAdmin.restoreTargetDbNameAuto: Auto-filled from the URI path. Edit if you need a different database name.
backupsAdmin.restoreTargetDbNameHint: Required when restoring to a custom cluster.
backupsAdmin.deleteDesc: Removes only the history entry. The remote database "{dbName}" stays untouched and must be cleaned manually if no longer needed.

setup.title: Set up Sentroy
setup.subtitle: The database hasn't been initialized yet. Choose how to start: seed defaults (creates an admin user + plans) or import an existing JSON dump.
setup.seedDesc: Create indexes, default plans (Free / Pro / Enterprise) and a fresh admin user.
setup.importDesc: Upload a previously exported backup JSON to restore an existing database.
setup.seedFormDesc: Provide the admin credentials you'll use to sign in afterward. They'll be created with the admin role and the default plan.
setup.adminPasswordHint: Use a strong password — this account has full admin access.
setup.setupTokenHint: Only required if a SETUP_TOKEN environment variable is set on the server. Leave empty otherwise.
setup.importFormDesc: Pick a JSON file exported from another Sentroy instance via Admin → Backups → Download.
setup.doneDesc: Setup is complete. You can now sign in with your admin credentials.

experimental.threejsVideosDesc: Pick a preset, tweak its colors and overlays, and record a short WebM clip. Configurations are saved to MongoDB so you can replay or duplicate them later.
experimental.emptyScenes: No saved scenes yet. Create your first one.
experimental.recordingDone: Recording finished — preview the file or download it.
experimental.savedScenesDesc: Open another scene, download its config, or remove it. Changes are persisted in MongoDB.
experimental.pickPresetDesc: Each preset is a curated scene — only the colors and a few knobs are exposed, so the look stays predictable.
experimental.noOverlays: No overlays yet — add a text or logo on top of the scene.
experimental.legacyScene: This scene was saved in the old format. Pick a preset to start over.
